  • Hide All Code

Synthetic disturbance–response series

Idealized climate driver & watershed response across a regime shift

nic (nic@cascadeclimate.org)

2026-07-17

Three idealized series, each time / climate / response. climate is the exogenous driver (∼precip); response (∼Q) is a lagged, log-linear transform of the realized noisy climate — log(response) = β·climate(t − lag) + noise, i.e. response = R0·exp(…) (the standard streamflow model: strictly positive, amplifies wet extremes, hydrograph-shaped). They differ in what the disturbance does to the terminal regime — Shift (permanent new regime), Desync (response decouples into noise), Return (transient excursion, recovers to regime n). The disturbance is a single time step marking the end of regime n — annotated (dashed line) on plots and encoded in the export as an ordered phase column with four categories: pre (regime n, before the disturbance), disturbance (that single step), transition (the adaptation / decoupling / excursion window), post (the settled terminal regime). Because log(response) is a clean lagged copy of climate, overlay plots show climate vs log(response) on one axis, with a companion real-units response panel for the hydrograph shape.

Parameters

P       <- 500                 # period, time steps. P≫lag ⇒ lag is a small phase fraction:
                               # log(response) is climate lag-shifted, so cor(climate, log(response))
                               # at the matching lag is ≈+1; contemporaneous cor ≈ cos(2π·lag/P)
                               # → +0.81 at lag 50, +0.99 at lag 10, attenuated by climate's noise.
Ac    <- 1.0                             # climate amplitude (exogenous driver, constant, no blend)
beta_n <- 1.0; lag_n <- 50               # regime n:   log-space sensitivity 1.0, long lag
beta_p <- 0.5; lag_p <- 10               # regime n+1: sensitivity halved, short lag
R0      <- 1.0                 # baseflow scale: response = R0·exp(logsig+noise). logR0=0 ⇒
                               # log(response) centred at 0, overlays climate directly.
lag_max <- max(lag_n, lag_p)   # warm-up length so climate(t−lag) is always defined
base_sd <- 0.05                # response baseline iid noise, LOG space (≈5% multiplicative)
clim_sd <- 0.25                # climate baseline iid noise, 25% of amp (noisier driver; linear)
dist_sd <- 0.30                # peak additional (disturbance) noise, LOG space (≈30% multiplicative)
LEN_REG   <- 2000              # regime span (= 4·P → 4 visible cycles/regime)
LEN_ADAPT <- 250              # adaptation / transition span

Engine

climate = Ac·sin(2πt/P) + noise is the exogenous driver, generated once. The response is a lagged, log-linear transform of the realized (noisy) climate — the standard streamflow model log(response) = β·climate(t − lag) + noise, i.e. response = R0·exp(…). This is strictly positive (soft baseflow floor, no clamp), amplifies wet extremes, and gives the flashy-peak / slow-recession hydrograph shape for free. Crucially climate’s 25% noise now propagates (lagged) into response — the response is a genuine product of climate, not an independent copy. A per-row morph weight w ∈ [0,1] (0 = regime n, 1 = n+1) drives transitions by crossfading two lagged copies of climate in log space: (1−w)·β_n·climate(t−lag_n) + w·β_p·climate(t−lag_p). Log space is linear, so the crossfade is smooth and free of spurious chirps: both regimes share P, so frequency returns exactly to P after the transition. The lag change itself is not frequency-flat — shifting the delay lag_n → lag_p over a finite window is a Doppler shift, f_out = f_in·(1 − dτ/dt), a transient excursion of magnitude ≈ Δlag/window ≈ 16% (Shift: one, speeds up; Return: two, out then back; Desync: none, w=0). This is the real kinematic signature of the lag change, not a numerical artifact. Because the lag is a plain index shift into the realized climate, climate is generated over an extended index (lag_max warm-up steps before t=1) so climate(t−lag) is always defined. Noise: climate carries a constant 25% linear baseline; response carries a constant ≈5% in log space (multiplicative — high flows noisier), plus a separate time-varying disturbance term in log space added to the response only, drawn on its own so it can be inspected in isolation (add_response; add_climate ≡ 0).

# clamped linear ramp: v0 for t≤t0, v1 for t≥t1
ramp <- function(t, t0, t1, v0, v1) v0 + pmin(pmax((t - t0) / (t1 - t0), 0), 1) * (v1 - v0)
# triangular weight: 0 at t0, 1 at peak, 0 at t1 (excursion / bump)
tri  <- function(t, t0, tpk, t1) ifelse(t <= tpk, ramp(t, t0, tpk, 0, 1), ramp(t, tpk, t1, 1, 0))

# ordered phase category for export: pre → disturbance (single step td) → transition → post.
# td = disturbance marker (end of regime n); t_trans_end = last step of the transition window.
phase_cat <- function(time, td, t_trans_end)
  factor(case_when(time <  td          ~ "pre",
                   time == td          ~ "disturbance",
                   time <= t_trans_end ~ "transition",
                   TRUE                ~ "post"),
         levels = c("pre", "disturbance", "transition", "post"))

# climate: exogenous driver, generated ONCE over an extended index so climate(t−lag) is
# always defined. idx runs (1−lag_max)…N; element for index i sits at position i + lag_max.
make_climate <- function(N) {
  idx <- seq.int(1 - lag_max, N)
  Ac * sin(2 * pi * idx / P) + rnorm(length(idx), 0, clim_sd)
}
# realized noisy climate at time t − lag (index shift into the extended vector)
clag <- function(climate_ext, t, lag) climate_ext[t - lag + lag_max]

# w: morph weight (0 = regime n, 1 = n+1), scalar or per-row vector.
# response = R0·exp(logsig + noise); logsig crossfades two lagged copies of the *realized*
# climate in log space (linear there → smooth; lag change adds a real transient freq
#   excursion f=f0·(1−dτ/dt), Doppler, not a numerical artifact). gain_r fades the signal
# (Desync only). All response noise is log space (multiplicative); climate is never disturbed.
build <- function(t, climate_ext, w, gain_r, add_sd_r, add_mu_r = 0) {
  n      <- length(t)
  add_r  <- rnorm(n, add_mu_r, add_sd_r)                       # disturbance noise (response, log space)
  logsig <- (1 - w) * beta_n * clag(climate_ext, t, lag_n) +
            w       * beta_p * clag(climate_ext, t, lag_p)     # crossfade of two lagged climate copies
  logresp <- log(R0) + gain_r * logsig + rnorm(n, 0, base_sd) + add_r
  tibble(
    time         = t,
    climate      = climate_ext[t + lag_max],                   # realized climate that later feeds clag
    response     = exp(logresp),                               # positive, right-skewed (hydrograph)
    log_response = logresp,                                    # clean lagged copy of climate (for overlay)
    add_climate  = 0, add_response = add_r                     # disturbance adds nothing to climate
  )
}

# consistent palettes: line colours (climate / log(response) / response) + phase-band fills
series_col <- c(climate = "grey15", `log(response)` = "#6a51a3", response = "#6a51a3")
phase_fill <- c(n = "#9ecae1", adapt = "#fcbba1", np1 = "#fdae6b")   # light blue / red / orange

# long frame overlaying climate and log(response) — both ≈ ±β, lag-shifted, same scale
overlay_long <- function(df)
  pivot_longer(df, c(climate, log_response), names_to = "var") %>%
    mutate(var = recode(var, log_response = "log(response)"))

plot_series <- function(df, title, xlim = NULL, vlines = numeric()) {
  ggplot(overlay_long(df), aes(time, value, colour = var)) +
    { if (length(vlines)) geom_vline(xintercept = vlines, linetype = 2, colour = "grey45") } +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = series_col) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = xlim) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = "value", colour = NULL)
}

# full-series view: labelled phase-band backgrounds behind climate + log(response)
# phases: tibble(xmin, xmax, type ∈ names(phase_fill), label)
plot_full <- function(df, phases, title, vlines = numeric()) {
  lo <- overlay_long(df)
  rng <- range(lo$value); top <- rng[2] + 0.18 * diff(rng)
  ggplot() +
    geom_rect(data = phases, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = type),
              alpha = 0.40, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_text(data = phases, aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = top, label = label),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3, vjust = 1, colour = "grey25") +
    { if (length(vlines)) geom_vline(xintercept = vlines, linetype = 2, colour = "grey30") } +
    geom_line(data = lo, aes(time, value, colour = var), linewidth = 0.3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = phase_fill, guide = "none") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = series_col) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(rng[1], top)) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = "value (climate & log-response)", colour = NULL)
}

# real-units response panel: the exp-scale (positive, flashy-peaked) hydrograph with bands
plot_flow <- function(df, phases, title, vlines = numeric()) {
  top <- max(df$response) * 1.05
  ggplot() +
    geom_rect(data = phases, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = type),
              alpha = 0.40, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_text(data = phases, aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = top, label = label),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3, vjust = 1, colour = "grey25") +
    { if (length(vlines)) geom_vline(xintercept = vlines, linetype = 2, colour = "grey30") } +
    geom_line(data = df, aes(time, response), colour = series_col[["response"]], linewidth = 0.3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = phase_fill, guide = "none") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, top)) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = "response (flow, real units)")
}

plot_addnoise <- function(df, title, vlines = numeric()) {
  ggplot(pivot_longer(df, c(add_climate, add_response), names_to = "var") %>%
           mutate(var = sub("add_", "", var)),
         aes(time, value, colour = var)) +
    { if (length(vlines)) geom_vline(xintercept = vlines, linetype = 2, colour = "grey45") } +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.2) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = series_col) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time", y = "additional disturbance noise (log space)", colour = NULL)
}

Transfer function

The response is built from a single static map applied to the lagged, realized climate — response = R0·exp(β·climate(t − lag)) (multiplicative noise omitted here). The curve below is the climate → response relationship we picked: convex and strictly positive in real units (dry anomalies decay to a soft baseflow floor at R0; wet anomalies are amplified), and a straight line of slope β in log space — the sense in which the response is log-linear. The regime shift halves the slope (β: 1.0 → 0.5) and shortens the lag (50 → 10); everything else in the document is this map driven by the realized climate and morphed via w.

suppressMessages(library(patchwork))

# static transfer curve for each regime's sensitivity β (noise-free, lag folded in via climate_lag)
lab_n  <- sprintf("regime n  (beta %.1f, lag %d)",  beta_n, lag_n)
lab_p  <- sprintf("regime n+1 (beta %.1f, lag %d)", beta_p, lag_p)
tf <- expand.grid(x = seq(-1.8, 1.8, length.out = 400), beta = c(beta_n, beta_p))
tf$regime   <- factor(ifelse(tf$beta == beta_n, lab_n, lab_p), levels = c(lab_n, lab_p))
tf$response <- R0 * exp(tf$beta * tf$x)          # real units
tf$logresp  <- log(R0) + tf$beta * tf$x          # log space (straight, slope β)
tf_col <- setNames(c("#6a51a3", "#fd8d3c"), c(lab_n, lab_p))

base_layers <- list(geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 3, colour = "grey55"),
                    scale_colour_manual(values = tf_col))

pA <- ggplot(tf, aes(x, response, colour = regime)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = R0, linetype = 3, colour = "grey55") + base_layers +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("point", x = 0, y = R0, colour = "grey25", size = 1.8) +
  annotate("text", x = -1.78, y = R0, label = "baseflow R0", hjust = 0, vjust = -0.6,
           size = 3, colour = "grey30") +
  labs(title = "Real units:  response = R0 · exp(beta · climate_lag)",
       subtitle = "convex & strictly positive — dry → soft floor, wet → amplified",
       x = "climate anomaly at t - lag", y = "response (flow)", colour = NULL)

pB <- ggplot(tf, aes(x, logresp, colour = regime)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = log(R0), linetype = 3, colour = "grey55") + base_layers +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  labs(title = "Log space:  log(response) = beta · climate_lag  (straight, slope = beta)",
       subtitle = "the 'log-linear' relationship the response is built on; slope halves n -> n+1",
       x = "climate anomaly at t - lag", y = "log(response)", colour = NULL)

pA / pB

Static climate→response transfer function: response = R0·exp(β·climate_lag). Top: real units (convex, positive, amplifies wet extremes). Bottom: log space (straight line, slope = β). Noise omitted; the actual response adds multiplicative log-space noise and the lag.

Shift

Regime n → disturbance → adaptation (params blend n→n+1, additional response noise decays 30%→0) → regime n+1. Settles permanently in the new regime.

N   <- 2 * LEN_REG + LEN_ADAPT          # 4250
t   <- seq_len(N)
td  <- LEN_REG                          # disturbance marker (end of regime n)
a0  <- td + 1; a1 <- td + LEN_ADAPT     # adaptation window [2001, 2250]
climate_ext <- make_climate(N)          # exogenous driver (extended for the lag warm-up)

# morph n → n+1 confined to the adaptation window (blends sensitivity + lag together)
w <- ramp(t, td, a1, 0, 1)

# additional disturbance noise on response: 30% at disturbance → 0 by regime n+1 (log space)
add_sd_r <- (t >= a0 & t <= a1) * dist_sd * ramp(t, a0, a1, 1, 0)

d_shift <- build(t, climate_ext, w, gain_r = 1, add_sd_r = add_sd_r) %>%
  mutate(phase = phase_cat(time, td, a1))   # pre | disturbance (td) | transition (adaptation) | post (regime n+1)
library(patchwork)
ph_shift <- tibble(xmin = c(1, a0, a1 + 1), xmax = c(td, a1, N), type = c("n", "adapt", "np1"),
                   label = c("Regime n", "Adaptation", "Regime n+1"))
plot_series(d_shift, "Regime n — climate & log(response), response lags by 50", xlim = c(1, 2 * P)) /
plot_series(d_shift, "Adaptation — lag/sensitivity morph + decaying disturbance noise",
            xlim = c(td - 2 * P, a1 + 2 * P), vlines = td) /
plot_full(d_shift, ph_shift, "Full series — climate & log(response), phases shaded (dashed = disturbance)", vlines = td) /
plot_flow(d_shift, ph_shift, "Response in real units — exp-scale hydrograph (amplitude halves post)", vlines = td) /
plot_addnoise(d_shift, "Additional disturbance noise — response 30%→0; climate none", vlines = td)

write_csv(d_shift %>% transmute(time, climate = round(climate, 6), response = round(response, 6), phase),
          file.path(OUT_DIR, "synthetic_shift.csv"))

Desync

Regime n → disturbance → the response decouples: its log-signal gain decays 1→0 while the additional (log-space) disturbance noise grows, so the terminal response is lognormal noise whose log-mean and log-variance match regime n. The match is log-space only: regime n is exp(sinusoid) (a Bessel-I₀ distribution) while the terminal block is exp(Gaussian) (lognormal), so at equal log-variance their raw-unit variance still differs — terminal ≈ +40% here. Compare in log space. Climate keeps oscillating (constant 25% noise) — a persistent driver the watershed no longer tracks coherently.

N   <- 2 * LEN_REG + LEN_ADAPT          # 4250
t   <- seq_len(N)
td  <- LEN_REG
a1  <- td + LEN_ADAPT                    # transition [2001, 2250]
climate_ext <- make_climate(N)          # exogenous driver (extended for the lag warm-up)

# terminal LOG-RESPONSE moments to match, estimated from climate_ext's pre-region BEFORE
# building the response (add_* are inputs to build(), so deriving them from its output is
# circular). Regime-n log-signal = beta_n·climate(t−lag_n); add base noise in quadrature.
# NB sd_lr ≈ beta_n·sd(clag): the marginal spread of the regime-n log-signal.
logsig_pre <- beta_n * clag(climate_ext, seq_len(td), lag_n)
mu_lr <- mean(logsig_pre); sd_lr <- sqrt(var(logsig_pre) + base_sd^2)

gain_r   <- ramp(t, td, a1, 1, 0)                              # log-signal fades, 0 after
# additional (log-space) noise grows so terminal log-response marginal-spread-matches regime n
add_sd_r <- ramp(t, td, a1, 0, sqrt(sd_lr^2 - base_sd^2))
add_mu_r <- ramp(t, td, a1, 0, mu_lr)                          # drift to regime-n log-response mean (≈0)

# climate untouched: clean sine + constant 25% noise; only response decouples into noise.
# w = 0 throughout (no morph); decoupling is carried entirely by the fading gain_r. Terminal
# response is lognormal noise whose LOG-moments match regime n. NB match is log-space ONLY:
# regime n is exp(sinusoid) (Bessel I0), terminal is exp(Gaussian) (lognormal), so raw-unit
# variance still differs (terminal ~+40%) at equal log-variance — see desync-check.
d_desync <- build(t, climate_ext, 0, gain_r = gain_r, add_sd_r = add_sd_r, add_mu_r = add_mu_r) %>%
  mutate(phase = phase_cat(time, td, a1))   # pre | disturbance (td) | transition (decoupling) | post (regime n+1 noise)
# design goal: terminal LOG-RESPONSE moments ≈ regime n log-response moments.
# NB by-construction identity — add_sd_r/add_mu_r were *set* from mu_lr/sd_lr, so this
# confirms the wiring reproduces them, it is NOT an independent validation of the design.
# Linear-space row shown for contrast: it does NOT match (sd_term ≈ 1.2× sd_reg_n) — regime n
# is exp(sinusoid), terminal is exp(Gaussian); equal log-variance ≠ equal raw variance.
term <- d_desync %>% filter(time > a1)
tibble(space      = c("log", "linear"),
       mean_reg_n = c(mu_lr,           mean(exp(logsig_pre) * R0)),
       mean_term  = c(mean(log(term$response)), mean(term$response)),
       sd_reg_n   = c(sd_lr,           sd(R0 * exp(beta_n * clag(climate_ext, seq_len(td), lag_n) +
                                                   rnorm(td, 0, base_sd)))),
       sd_term    = c(sd(log(term$response)),   sd(term$response)))
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   space  mean_reg_n mean_term sd_reg_n sd_term
##   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 log      -0.00648  -0.00359    0.753   0.752
## 2 linear    1.30      1.32       0.939   1.13
ph_desync <- tibble(xmin = c(1, td + 1, a1 + 1), xmax = c(td, a1, N), type = c("n", "adapt", "np1"),
                    label = c("Regime n", "Transition", "Regime n+1 (noise)"))
plot_series(d_desync, "Regime n — climate & log(response)", xlim = c(1, 2 * P)) /
plot_series(d_desync, "Transition — log(response) decays into matched-variance noise; climate persists",
            xlim = c(td - 2 * P, a1 + 3 * P), vlines = td) /
plot_full(d_desync, ph_desync, "Full series — climate & log(response), phases shaded (dashed = disturbance)", vlines = td) /
plot_flow(d_desync, ph_desync, "Response in real units — hydrograph decays to lognormal noise", vlines = td) /
plot_addnoise(d_desync, "Additional disturbance noise — response grows to regime-n log-variance; climate none", vlines = td)

write_csv(d_desync %>% transmute(time, climate = round(climate, 6), response = round(response, 6), phase),
          file.path(OUT_DIR, "synthetic_desync.csv"))

Return

Regime n → disturbance → single excursion toward n+1 that peaks then decays straight back to regime n. No sustained n+1. Additional disturbance noise strong at the disturbance, → 0 by the time the system re-settles in regime n.

N   <- 2 * LEN_REG + 2 * LEN_ADAPT      # 4500
t   <- seq_len(N)
td  <- LEN_REG                          # disturbance
pk  <- td + LEN_ADAPT                   # 2250 max excursion (params == n+1)
e1  <- td + 2 * LEN_ADAPT               # 2500 back to regime n
climate_ext <- make_climate(N)          # exogenous driver (extended for the lag warm-up)

w   <- tri(t, td, pk, e1)               # 0 →1 at peak →0 (drives the sensitivity/lag blend)

# additional disturbance noise on response: 30% at disturbance → 0 by re-settle (log space)
add_sd_r <- (t > td & t <= e1) * dist_sd * ramp(t, td + 1, e1, 1, 0)

d_return <- build(t, climate_ext, w, gain_r = 1, add_sd_r = add_sd_r) %>%
  mutate(phase = phase_cat(time, td, e1))   # pre | disturbance (td) | transition (excursion) | post (back to regime n)
# excursion peaks at n+1 params then returns; no sustained n+1 regime → both ends are regime n
ph_return <- tibble(xmin = c(1, td + 1, e1 + 1), xmax = c(td, e1, N), type = c("n", "adapt", "n"),
                    label = c("Regime n", "Excursion", "Regime n"))
plot_series(d_return, "Regime n — climate & log(response)", xlim = c(1, 2 * P)) /
plot_series(d_return, "Excursion — morph to n+1 at peak, back to n",
            xlim = c(td - 2 * P, e1 + 2 * P), vlines = c(td, pk)) /
plot_full(d_return, ph_return, "Full series — climate & log(response), phases shaded (dashed = disturbance, peak)", vlines = c(td, pk)) /
plot_flow(d_return, ph_return, "Response in real units — hydrograph excursion then re-settle", vlines = c(td, pk)) /
plot_addnoise(d_return, "Additional disturbance noise — response 30%→0 over excursion; climate none", vlines = c(td, pk))

write_csv(d_return %>% transmute(time, climate = round(climate, 6), response = round(response, 6), phase),
          file.path(OUT_DIR, "synthetic_return.csv"))
